reason_for_decision: The article gives a clear beginner explanation of beamforming, separates it from beam steering, and adds enough architecture and trade-off context to pass without revision.
strengths: The article correctly frames beamforming as broader than beam steering and keeps that distinction consistent.
It turns abstract array-processing ideas into practical system questions about sidelobes, calibration, and architecture.
The analog, sub-array, and digital comparison adds useful beginner decision value without becoming overly mathematical.
summary_assessment: This is a strong Foundation article: it is readable, technically grounded, and useful enough to pass without revision.
